easy.title: Create a Formatting Showcase Paragraph
easy.description: Write a single paragraph that demonstrates at least 8 different text formatting elements. Use each element in a way that makes semantic sense. Include: <strong>, <em>, <mark>, <small>, <del>, <ins>, <sub>, <sup>, <code>, and <abbr>. The paragraph should be a coherent sentence about web development.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Formatting Showcase</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Write a paragraph with 8+ formatting elements -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution:
Text Formatting Showcase

When learning HTML, it is essential to understand semantic markup. The <mark> element highlights key terms, while small text is for fine print. Prices often show $50 $35 for discounts. Chemical formulas use H2O, and math uses E=mc2. These elements give meaning to your content.

medium.title: Build a Text Formatting Reference Page
medium.description: Create a comprehensive reference page that catalogs all 20+ text formatting elements. For each element, show: the element name, a demo of its rendered output, a description of its semantic meaning, and a code example. Organize elements into categories: Semantic Importance, Technical, Editorial Changes, and Citations/Quotes.
medium.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Text Formatting Reference</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a comprehensive formatting reference -->
</body>
</html>
medium.solution:
HTML Text Formatting Reference

Semantic & Importance | Technical | Editorial Changes | Citations & Quotes

Semantic & Importance Elements

<strong>

Strong importance — indicates serious importance. Screen readers use different emphasis.

<strong>Warning!</strong>

<em>

Emphasized text — indicates stress emphasis that can change sentence meaning.

<em>really</em>

<mark>

Highlighted text — marks content for reference, like search keywords.

<mark>key result</mark>

<small>

Fine print and disclaimers — for legal text, copyright, and side comments.

<small>© 2026</small>

Technical Elements

<code>

Inline code snippets in monospace font.

<code>function()</code>

<kbd>

Press Ctrl + S to save.

<kbd>Ctrl</kbd>

<samp>

Sample output: Hello, World!

<samp>Hello</samp>

<var>

Variable: userName = value.

<var>userName</var>

<abbr>

HTML is the foundation of the web.

<abbr title="HyperText Markup Language">HTML</abbr>

<sub> & <sup>

H2O and E=mc2

H<sub>2</sub>O and E=mc<sup>2</sup>

Editorial Changes

<del> & <ins>

Price: $29.99 $19.99

<del>$29.99</del> <ins>$19.99</ins>

<s>

This offer has expired.

<s>Expired content</s>

Citations & Quotes

<q>

He said, This is a short quote.

<q>This is a short quote.</q>

<cite>

The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald

<cite>The Great Gatsby</cite>

<dfn>

HTML is a markup language for web pages.

<dfn>HTML</dfn>

© 2026 Text Formatting Reference

hard.title: Build a Document Revision History Page
hard.description: Create a 'Document Revision History' page that demonstrates editorial formatting elements. Show a document with multiple revisions marked up using <del>, <ins>, <s>, and <time> elements. Include: a table of changes (version, date, author, changes), the actual document body showing revisions with strikethrough for removed text and underline for added text, a comments/discussion section using <ins> for new comments and <del> for resolved comments, and a diff-style summary at the bottom showing added/removed word counts using <ins> and <del>.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Document Revision History</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a revision history page with editorial formatting -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
Project Proposal: Website Redesign

Status: Approved Pending Review

Last Updated: June 10, 2026

Revision History

Version Date Author Changes
1.0 May 1, 2026 John Doe Initial draft
1.1 May 15, 2026 Jane Smith Added budget section; removed outdated timeline
2.0 June 1, 2026 John Doe Major revision: added new scope; old appendix removed
2.1 June 10, 2026 Jane Smith Final approval notes

Document Body (with Changes Tracked)

1. Executive Summary

This proposal outlines the comprehensive redesign of the company website. The current site is outdated and needs modernization. The primary goal is to improve user engagement and conversion rates.

2. Scope of Work

The project includes the following phases:

  • Basic HTML/CSS redesign Full front-end overhaul with modern framework
  • Responsive mobile-first design
  • Contact form update Complete contact management system

3. Budget

Estimated budget: $15,000 $18,500 (revised for expanded scope).

Item Cost
Design$5,000
Development$8,000 $10,000
Testing$2,500
Project Management$1,000

4. Timeline

The original timeline of 6 weeks is no longer feasible. Updated timeline: 8 weeks, starting July 1, 2026.

Comments & Discussion

Comment from Jane Smith May 16, 2026

I have added the budget section. Please review the updated figures.

Comment from John Doe May 20, 2026

The budget numbers look incorrect. I will recalculate and update.

Budget has been corrected. Ready for final review.

Diff Summary

+ Added: 245 words across 3 sections
- Removed: 89 words across 2 sections
~ Deprecated: 1 section (Timeline)
